batch_summary: In Monica's kitchen, Joey opens up about his painful unrequited feelings for an actress co-star. The mood shifts when Ross arrives with news that their unpleasant Aunt Sylvia has passed away. Monica is ecstatic to learn she has inherited her aunt's prized dollhouse, which she was never allowed to play with as a child. After Phoebe shares a sad childhood story about only having a barrel, Monica kindly invites her to play with the dollhouse, while Ross recalls their aunt's cruel past demands.
cumulative_summary: In the apartment kitchen, Joey shares his rare experience of unrequited love, explaining how painful it is to watch his actress co-star go home with their director. Chandler, Phoebe, and Monica listen, with Chandler finding humor in Joey finally experiencing romantic rejection. The conversation shifts when Ross arrives to announce the passing of their Aunt Sylvia. Rather than mourning, Monica is overjoyed because her notoriously cruel aunt has left her a highly coveted childhood dollhouse that she was never permitted to play with. When Phoebe reveals she had nothing but a literal barrel to play with as a child, Monica generously promises to let Phoebe share the dollhouse. Ross then humorously reminds Monica of their aunt's harsh terms for the dollhouse in the past, noting she once demanded Monica lose twenty pounds just to play with it.

batch_summary: Monica makes a dark joke about her late aunt's weight before the scene shifts to Rachel's office at Bloomingdale's. Chandler walks Rachel back from lunch, where he meets her boss, Joanna. Despite Chandler's goofy humor, Joanna is highly intrigued and asks Rachel if he is single. Rachel offers to set them up, while Joanna casually insults her assistant, Sophie. Later, back at the apartment, Monica proudly displays her newly inherited, elaborate dollhouse to the group.
